topic: Someone receives a friend request from a childhood bully
raw_output:
[story 1]

The notification popped up on my phone, a jarring intrusion into the otherwise quiet afternoon. My jaw tightened almost imperceptibly as I tapped the screen, my thumb hovering over the 'Accept' button with palpable reluctance. It was him. Mark. The memory of his sneering face from the seventh grade, the way he’d always managed to make me feel small, washed over me like cold water. I stared at the profile picture—a forced, overly casual grin—and felt a familiar knot tighten in my stomach. Why would he even bother? A slow, deliberate swipe of my finger across the screen, accepting the request, felt like conceding a small, pointless battle.

[story 2]

I nearly dropped my coffee mug. The message thread opened, revealing his name at the top. My breath hitched, a shallow, quick intake of air. Every muscle in my shoulders immediately tensed, pulling my jacket tighter around my chest. *Seriously?* The thought echoed in my head, sharp and cutting. I scrolled through his sparse profile, cataloging the superficial details, each piece of information fueling a growing sense of irritation. I didn't want this. I didn't want any interaction with that particular brand of manufactured familiarity. With a sigh that was more of a sharp expulsion of air than actual sound, I navigated to the settings, ready to block him before I even considered replying.

[story 3]

My gaze snagged on the incoming request, and a distinct clenching sensation spread through my hand. It was a low-grade tension, like a wire pulled taut just before snapping. I leaned back in my chair, crossing my arms over my chest, adopting a posture that was deliberately closed off. Seeing his name pop up felt like an unwelcome intrusion into my carefully constructed peace. I didn't even need to click it immediately; the sheer audacity of the gesture was enough to make me want to physically recoil. I let the notification sit there, a small, irritating speck on my screen, contemplating the sheer waste of my time in acknowledging it.
